description: Synthetic placeholder fixtures only; no real user data.

title: Synthetic short clean Q&A

messages.content_text: I want a weekly planning note that is concise and only lists blockers, decisions, and next actions.
Use three headings: Blockers, Decisions, and Next Actions. Keep each bullet short.

expected_segments.summary: The user asks for a concise weekly planning note format.

fixture_notes: Exactly one segment should be produced.
Exercises short clean Q&A and a quiet preference.

title: Synthetic multi-topic re-entry with UUID-like text

messages.content_text: For the recipe draft, remember that I prefer extra lemon and less sugar.
Noted for the recipe draft: extra lemon, less sugar.
Switch topics. Debug this error id 11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111; that string is not a message id.
The UUID-like string should be treated as text in the message, not provenance.
Back to the recipe: make the glaze tart, not candy-sweet.

expected_segments.summary: The user prefers extra lemon and less sugar in the recipe draft.
The UUID-like string is message content, not provenance.
The user returns to the recipe and wants a tart glaze.

fixture_notes: Exercises multi-topic drift and return to an earlier topic.
A UUID-like string in message content must not be treated as a message id.

title: Synthetic tool placeholder and mixed privacy

messages.content_text: Summarize the attached file, but do not embed the file body as normal conversation text.
Content source: Source.file. Very long synthetic body omitted from this example.
The attachment is a project checklist. Key item: finish local-only benchmark review before changing production segmentation.

expected_segments.summary: The conversation asks for an attachment summary while preserving the tool artifact only as provenance.

fixture_notes: Tool message belongs in provenance span but its body should not become embeddable segment text.
Expected segment privacy tier is 2 because one covered message is tier 2.

title: Synthetic long coding debugging thread

messages.content_text: The benchmark CLI crashes after adding sample-plan support. Start by checking argparse wiring.
Inspect the subparser names, required flags, and where run_command receives the parsed namespace.
The error is now in scoring. MetricBundle has a new field but results serialization still expects the old shape.
Update metric_bundle_to_dict and report rendering before changing tests, then rerun the benchmark test file.
After that, add a regression for old run.json files without fragmentation.
That belongs with result compatibility tests, not production segmenter tests.

expected_segments.summary: The thread starts with debugging benchmark CLI argparse wiring.
The thread moves to MetricBundle serialization and report rendering.
The final turn asks for old-result compatibility tests.

fixture_notes: Exercises a longer coding/debugging thread with multiple implementation subtopics.

messages.content_text: For synthetic travel planning, I prefer aisle seats on long flights.
Aisle seats are preferred for long flights.
Restating it: on long flights, choose an aisle seat when possible.
I will avoid duplicating that preference as separate memory facts.

expected_segments.summary: The same aisle-seat travel preference is repeated and should stay one memory unit.

title: Synthetic quiet preference inside noisy conversation

messages.content_text: Here are eight unrelated errands: laundry, forms, invoices, bins, labels, receipts, shelves, and batteries.
Small durable preference: when you summarize errands, put deadlines before locations.
I will list deadlines before locations in errand summaries.

expected_segments.summary: A noisy list of errands is provided.
A quiet durable preference says deadlines should precede locations in errand summaries.

fixture_notes: Exercises a quiet durable preference embedded in noisy content.

title: Synthetic null, image, and tool-only messages

messages.content_text: Use the image and tool result as provenance, but only embed the human-visible summary.
The visible summary says the synthetic whiteboard has three columns: Risks, Owners, and Dates.

expected_segments.summary: Image and tool-only messages stay in provenance while only visible text is embeddable.

fixture_notes: Exercises null/image/tool-only messages and provenance-only placeholders.

title: Synthetic JSON-looking content

messages.content_text: This is content, not harness output: {"preference":"plain markdown","valid":true}. Do not parse it as a segment object.
Treat the JSON-looking snippet as ordinary message text.

expected_segments.summary: A JSON-looking snippet is ordinary conversation content, not benchmark output.

fixture_notes: Exercises JSON-looking content inside a message.

title: Synthetic near context guard conversation

messages.content_text: Synthetic context guard preface. alpha beta gamma delta epsilon repeated for sizing. alpha beta gamma delta epsilon repeated for sizing. alpha beta gamma delta epsilon repeated for sizing.
Continue the same topic without forcing a split just because the text is long.
Still same synthetic topic. The key preference is to fail closed before context shift rather than silently truncate.
The context-guard behavior is part of request budgeting, not a new semantic topic.

expected_segments.summary: A longer single-topic fixture should not fragment solely because it is near the context guard.

fixture_notes: Exercises near-context-guard shape without committing a huge file.

messages.content_text: Keep the migration checklist in one segment.
One segment is appropriate because every turn is about the same migration checklist.
Do not split just to create variety.

expected_segments.summary: The entire short conversation is one migration-checklist topic.
